etymology.description: semantic 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 將(將) (jiāng) -> thick liquid such as starch, broth
etymology.interpretation: In 漿(漿), the semantic component 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") indicates a liquid, while the phonetic component 將(將) (jiāng) suggests the pronunciation. This combination implies a thick or substantial liquid, leading to meanings like starch or broth, where a liquid base is thickened with other substances.
etymology.simplification: The traditional form 漿(漿) was simplified by reducing the component 水(水) to the simpler form 氵.

examples.chinese: 早上我喝了一杯豆浆(早上我喝了一杯豆漿)。
examples.pinyin: Zǎoshang wǒ hē le yī bēi dòujiāng.
examples.english: In the morning, I drank a cup of soybean milk.

examples.chinese: 粘稠的米浆(粘稠的米漿)非常适合这个菜(非常適合這個菜)。
examples.pinyin: Zhānchóu de mǐjiāng fēicháng shìhé zhège cài.
examples.english: The thick rice paste is perfect for this dish.

examples.chinese: 她喜欢用玉米浆(她喜歡用玉米漿)做汤(做湯)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān yòng yùmǐjiāng zuò tāng.
examples.english: She likes to use corn broth to make soup.
